title: Accessibility + readability pass (alt-text, colorblind-safe curves, reading level)
context: Each explainer should meet readability and accessibility standards so that the content is usable by clinicians and trainees with visual impairments, color vision deficiencies (colorblindness affects approximately 8% of men), and varying reading fluency. This task defines the accessibility and readability standards for the project, applies them to the pilot explainers (hr-004, km-006), and produces a reusable checklist and tooling guidance so that all subsequent explainers can self-assess. It depends on the pilot explainers being drafted so there is content to assess.
objective: Define and document the project's accessibility and readability standards (alt-text requirements, colorblind-safe figure palettes, and target reading level), apply them to the pilot explainers, and publish a reusable accessibility checklist and tooling guidance for authors.
acceptanceCriteria: The accessibility standards document specifies: alt-text requirements (every figure must have descriptive alt-text that conveys the statistical message of the figure, not just its visual description); colorblind-safe palette requirements (survival curves must use a palette distinguishable under deuteranopia and protanopia; specific approved palettes listed); and target reading level (e.g. Flesch-Kincaid grade level appropriate for a clinician/trainee audience).
The pilot explainers (hr-004, km-006) are reviewed against the standards and any gaps (missing alt-text, non-compliant color choices, above-target reading level) are documented and remediated.
A reusable per-explainer accessibility checklist (covering the three standards above) is committed and referenced from the authoring template's accessibility field.
Tooling guidance is provided (e.g. how to check reading level, which palette tool to use, how to validate alt-text) so authors can self-assess without needing a specialist for every explainer.
The plain-language/accessibility reviewer has approved the standards document and confirmed the pilot explainers meet them.
DCO signed-off; the standards document and checklist are committed to the project repo.
resources: tasks/stats-for-clinicians-hr-004.json — pilot explainer for standards application
tasks/stats-for-clinicians-km-006.json — pilot explainer for standards application
WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility guidelines; colorblind-safe palette resources (e.g. Okabe-Ito, ColorBrewer)
output: A committed accessibility and readability standards document, a per-explainer accessibility checklist, tooling guidance for authors, and a record confirming the pilot explainers (hr-004, km-006) meet the standards.
